post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 188: Geometry Dash Mafia!

If you are not currently alive in this game, please do not post in this thread. Visit this thread to sign up for future games.

Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Flavor
The world of Geometry Dash is an ever shifting landscape which players have to battle their way through. This gauntlet is divided into regions called “levels”. However, recently the first 14 levels seemed to have gained sentience. And not all of them are happy with each other’s existence. Some of the levels secretly decide to wipe out the others, claiming that they and they alone should be Geometry Dash.


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification. Questions asked in-thread will only be answered in-PM, if at all, unless I specifically believe it is something worth telling the general public.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderators, unless you are specifically allowed to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.


Voting/Hanging
  • This game is based on deadline hang. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is hanged. Ties will be explained in the next section. Starting Day 2, majority hanging is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority hang is not reached, deadline hang occurs.
  • If there is a tie vote at the end of the day between two players, the result of it will be randomized. However, if there are an equal number of votes for No Hang and a player, No Hang will be the result.
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this - see below.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is. If we do not notice that you fakevoted, it may be counted as a real vote, so it is a good idea to PM me every time you intend to fakevote.
  • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang". (Note: This is not the same thing as unvoting.)
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting to not hang, then nobody will be hanged. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight (the time between the end of deadline and the moderator's "do not post" post), players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post an end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • If you have read the entire OP and are ready to totally participate in this game, please make a post in the thread containing any one of the following phrases: “triple spike”, “Nerf gun”, “2.2”, or “98%” (these are all case sensitive, ignoring quotes).


Activity
  • Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • Activity rule for this game: While not on V/LA, you are expected to make at least one game-advancing post between every activity check, which will be done roughly every 48 hours. If you miss one activity check, you will get an in-game prod; if you miss two activity checks in a row, you will get a PM prod; if you miss three activity checks in a row, you will be replaced. Do not try to abuse this by saying "activity post" every two days - one of us will warn or replace you if we feel you are posting purely to bypass this requirement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, and SE’s/IC's up to one. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force-replaced, depending on the scenario. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (benjaminsz) am the Moderator of Game 188. I am currently accepting any requests (from dead players or otherwise) to be my Co-Moderator.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not do things that have the effect of impersonating a moderator. For the most part, though, posting in colors is fine.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup
This game will use a closed setup. The game is not BM, there are no BM mechanics, and I (the moderator) will never lie to you, in your PM’s, the thread, or this OP. There are only a few things I can tell you about the setup for certain, however. First, all cops are sane. Second, there is at least one third party role. Flavor-wise, each of you will take on the role of one of the first 14 Robtop Official levels of Geometry Dash, of whom some wish to destroy the others. You may roleclaim, of course, but you may only give mechanical detail. You may not flavorclaim, but you are allowed to claim the difficulty of your level, which is in your Role PM. You can also see the difficulty of all levels on the RobTop official website or by Googling.


Alive (7)
  1. zdtsd+
  2. SmartZX
  3. MY-2
  4. RTG++
  5. AhirGauss xzhang
  6. avanders
  7. kittenwarrior litee


Dead (7)
  1. lkarhat (One-Shot Bulletproof Townie)
  2. Madelyn (Vanilla Townie)
  3. kevinmathz (Vanilla Townie)
  4. GeneralCobra19+ (Vanilla Townie)
  5. mathjay+ (One-Shot Bulletproof Serial Killer)
  6. NDMath (Vanilla Townie)
  7. Bronzefur (Town Cop)


Significant Events
  • The Moderator, benjaminsz, beats Theory of Everything II, which is not in the game, 9/25
  • End of Day One, 9/28
  • End of Night One, 10/1
  • End of Day Two, 10/12
  • End of Night Two, 10/15
  • End of Day Three, 10/22
  • End of Night Three, 10/25
  • End of Day Four, 11/4
  • End of Night 4, 11/8
  • End of Game, 11/17


Warnings
  • AhirGauss (1): OOT
  • xzhang (2): Severe claim violation


Lastly, here
is a playlist that has every Geometry Dash level in it, to help get you inspired! It is strongly recommended to watch at least your own level, paying attention to both the level and the music, in order to better understand your role PM and also get you nice and hyped up for the game!

Congratulations to the mafia (RTG, SmartZX and litee) for winning the game!

Full role PM list:

Role PM's
Stereo Madness: My-2

Hi. You’re Stereo Madness. You’re the first level of Geometry Dash, responsible for easing players into the game (of course, you’re a Very Easy level), and you introduce such core mechanics as cube and ship sections. However, there’s nothing else really special about you, so you’re a Vanilla Townie. Good luck.

Back on Track: GeneralCobra19

Hi. You’re Back On Track. You’re the second level of Geometry Dash, a Very Easy level. You introduce jump pads to the game, but that’s not super impressive, so you’re just a Vanilla Townie. Good luck, you memelord of a level, you.

Polargeist: lkarhat

Hi. You’re Polargeist. You’re the third level of Geometry Dash and the first Easy level. You introduce jump orbs to the game, but what most remember you for is your incredible synchronisation with your music. You’re so in sync that it protects you somewhat, so you’re a One-Shot Bulletproof Townie. Good luck.

Dry Out: avanders

Hi. You’re Dry Out. You’re the fourth level of Geometry Dash, an Easy level. You introduce upside-down segments to the game, but you don’t really have anything else special about you, so you’re just a Vanilla Townie. Good luck.

Base After Base: kevinmathz

Hi. You’re Base After Base. You’re the fifth level of Geometry Dash, the first Hard level. You don’t really introduce much of anything new, and have repetitive music to boot, so we’re only making you a Vanilla Townie. Good luck.

Can’t Let Go: AhirGauss/xzhang

Hi. You’re Can’t Let Go. You’re the sixth level of Geometry Dash, a Hard level. You don’t really introduce much of anything new, so we’re only making you a Vanilla Townie. Good luck.

Jumper: NDMath

Hi. You’re Jumper. You’re the seventh level of Geometry Dash, the first Harder level. You don’t really introduce much of anything new, although you are the first level with an upside-down ship section together, so we’re only making you a Vanilla Townie. Good luck.

Time Machine: kittenwarrior/litee

Hi. You’re Time Machine. You’re the eighth level of Geometry Dash, a Harder level. You introduce backwards sections, among other things. The community is highly divided about your music; some people (myself included) think it’s epic, and others hate it. The one thing they can agree on is that no one likes you for your constant triple spikes. These perspectives from the community have driven you somewhat insane, so we’re making you a Mafia Goon, out for blood and wanting to destroy the levels who don’t know what it’s like to be hated by the community. You can chat with your partners, RTG (Cycles) and SmartZX (Clutterfunk), here. Good luck.

Cycles: RTG

Hi. You’re Cycles. You’re the ninth level of Geometry dash, a Harder level. You introduce gravity ball sections, among other things. Unfortunately, nobody likes your music, which has driven you somewhat insane. As a result, we’re making you a Mafia Goon, out for blood and wanting to destroy the levels who don’t know what it’s like to be hated by the community. You can chat with your partners, kittenwarrior (Time Machine) and SmartZX (Clutterfunk), here. Good luck.




xStep: zdtsd

Hi. You’re xStep. You’re the tenth level of Geometry Dash. You introduce gravity-switching jump pads, and you are the first level to really start to casually throw section portals every six seconds. These things, combined with being the first Insane level and coming after the somewhat unimpressive Cycles, mean that players tend to get stuck on you for way longer than expected. As a result, we’re making you a Town Jailkeeper. Good luck.

Clutterfunk: SmartZX

Hi. You’re Clutterfunk. You’re the eleventh level of Geometry dash, an Insane level and the first to contain small-size sections and gravity orbs. You are an ambitious and power-hungry level, known for already being harder than you should be. You’ve played on the anger of Time Machine and Cycles at their community hatred to bring them to your side. With their help, you will destroy the other weak, unsuspecting levels. By absorbing their power, you will become the Demon level you always deserved to be. But you don’t just have your minion levels at your disposal, no indeed. Your music is well known to the community to be epic and amazing, but it’s particularly harsh and irritating for a Geometry Dash level, and as players struggle against your outsize difficulty, it can start to get to them. This gives you the power to seriously mess with the other levels’ minds, so we’re making you a Mafia Roleblocker. You can chat with your partners, kittenwarrior (Time Machine) and RTG (Cycles), here. Good luck.

Theory of Everything: Bronzefur

Hi. You’re Theory of Everything. You’re the twelfth level of Geometry Dash, an Insane level. You don’t really introduce much of anything new, and difficulty-wise can seem like a bit of a step down after Clutterfunk. But you are the first level to come out of the gates with immediate orb spam, you introduce UFO sections to the game, and you have epic music. More importantly, you’re kind of the leader of the Innocent levels, and you are the Theory of Everything, after all, so you sort of know everything. As a result, we’re making you a Town Cop. Good luck.

Electroman Adventures: Madelyn

Hi. You’re Electroman Adventures. You’re the thirteenth level of Geometry Dash. You’re an Insane level, like all the others, but being sandwiched in between Theory of Everything and Clutterfunk on one side, and Clubstep on the other, you’re kind of overshadowed and don’t add much new stuff, and on top of that you come just as the novelty of Insane levels is beginning to wear off. As a result, we’re only making you a Vanilla Townie. Good luck.

Clubstep: Mathjay

Hi. You’re Clubstep. You’re the fourteenth level of Geometry Dash and the first Demon level, and you secretly have believed all along that this makes you superior. You know that survival is about adaptation or death, and the Demon level master race must triumph. You’ve been waiting to begin the rampage for a long time, and the insurgency of Clutterfunk and his gang is just the opportunity you’ve been waiting for. With your Demon powers, you will crush the first thirteen levels - they are too weak and puny to stand up to you. Your epic music also has a part near the beginning which is very dissonant and thrown out of synchronization, somewhat protecting you from attack. All of this combines to make you a One-Shot Bulletproof Serial Killer. Good luck.

Feedback:
zdtsd first cause I have a lot to say about him

zdtsd
Ok, I love you, man, but you really jobbed this game. Scumhunting on your part was next to nonexistent. The mafia were the ones who got SK hung, ironically enough, and your big push was a LyLo mishang. Said mishang had minimal evidence, and sure the maf helped it along, but you gave it legitimacy for people like My-2 to vote for it. You also didn't realize jay was probably SK because a BP already died and SK is the only antitown role that's often BP. You also somehow went along with RTG being town because he hung jay, even though maf want to kill SK. The rest of town had excuses for those last two (mostly being bad at the game), but you should know better. NA's were almost better, but weren't. Multiple nights you came up with various levels of IQ plays from 0 IQ to 10,000,000 IQ, and every time you picked the one and only play that wasn't a good one. (Seriously, the first time you did that, EVERY SINGLE OPTION other than the one you went for would have stopped the kill and probably given you info enough to get much better dayplay for the rest of the game.) In summary, maybe pretend this game never happened when you apply IC. We all still think you're a genius at heart.


maf next cause they won

RTG
Next level scum play as expected. IDK how everyone (especially zd) just kind of locktowned you for killing SK even though maf wants to kill SK, but good scumplay all around in terms of flying under the radar without appearing scummy. Also great job leading your teammates and helping them be good mafioso, as well as picking all NA's very intelligently.


SmartZX and litee since I pretty much have the same feedback for both of them
So you guys absolutely played mafia adequately, but you could have done considerably better. Lurking all game is a solid gameplan for newbie mafia, but if you hadn't had RTG, or ZD had played better, or a million other things, you could easily have been found out. I highly recommend trying to be more assertive; imitate your town playstyle as much as possible and aggressively pursue mishangs. I find that instead of calculating all of your posts, it's easiest to simply make all of your posts as if you're town, and determine only your final votes in a day from a mafia perspective. You'll probably be found out and hung quickly the first time or two that you play this way, I know I was. But over time you'll improve, and you'll never really do that playing the way you did this game. Great game from both of you for your experience level, but you can definitely do better in future scum games.


other players who survived until the endgame next

My-2 and avanders cause again I have similar feedback for them
Sorry to you two, but I can never figure out how to give feedback for people at your level of experience. I don't know how to help you improve the level of your play from "experienced newbie" to "SE-worthy" because I myself struggled in that rut for a very long time. Maybe others have useful feedback for you, but all I can say is that you just need to somehow figure out how to get better at the game. I'm still not sure how I did it. Maybe one thing I can say for My-2 is that you need to learn not to sheep people. ALWAYS think 100% for yourself, NEVER go along with something just because someone else said it until you have thought it through completely yourself, and NEVER, EVER assume anyone is town unless they mechanically have to be.


xzhang
Man, you're the one I pity in this game. You didn't really do anything wrong, and yet town's most experienced player AND maf lead a mishang of you - at LyLo no less! You do absolutely need to contribute more to avoid stuff like that happening in the future. It can be as simple as posting reads every now and then, with reasoning why you believe what you do. You'll learn with experience how to really scumhunt, but for now, that should do. Also, as I said for My-2, DON'T sheep people. You'll never learn to contribute if you only follow other people's contributions.


mathjay and bronzefur next for being PR's

mathjay
Please don't miss your N1 kill.

I really can't say anything else, because who knows what would have happened if you'd killed, say, RTG? Or Bronzefur? You just got so unlucky this game - both to miss your N1 kill and get copped so quickly. Your dayplay was excellent, I would probably have TR'ed you no problem, maf only caught onto you because RTG is a genius (and ofc the cop check). And there are any number of scenarios where that wouldn't have mattered (e.g. maf all dies before they can shoot you twice, bronzefur never cops you, you shoot RTG N1, etc.). Basically, better luck next time.


Bronzefur
You actually played pretty well for first time cop, getting jay and such. If zd had used his NA's even slightly more effectively the two of you could probably have lead town to victory over the maf after getting SK so quickly. Finding RTG would have been hard, but I can see a LyLo that's just you two and him, and you obviously would hang him and win it. So just overall, good PR play. That said, you absolutely need to contribute more in-thread if you're going to succeed as VT and improve as PR. See my feedback for xzhang for more on that.

Thanks benj for the well modded game and your feedback for everyone is basically what I would say too. For GC, you gave off super townie vibes so good as town but I feel like you didn’t really try to contribute which I think could make you better as a town player. Madelyn, you’re like the perfect N1 kill for scum. An insightful enough player for a nice player for mafia to get rid of, but you don’t draw enough attention to yourself to warrant a protection. Your play this game is actually a perfect game to play if you’re scum, but if town is a N1 kill a lot of the time.

Also, to mathjay, I did seem to get scummy vibes off you D1. When you were coming after me for my reads D1 I got scummy vibes cause it felt like you were just trying to go after a high profile player which I’ve seen many times by scum in the past. Then when I called you out for it you backed off making you seem more scummy to me. However I didn’t want to bring more attention to myself by coming after you for it since you’re a good enough player where there could have been a chance where the hang ended up being on me. Finally, just want to put it out there that I’m still a perfect 3 for 3 as scum. There were tells that people should have been able to catch on to like my almost total lack of scumhunting contribution, but it really helped that no one was really doing any scumhunting either.

post_rendered:
oh btw was just reading this and saw RTG's thing on mad being an N1 kill

so here is a bit of my philosophy on townplay: Always try to contribute and scumhunt, but also it's good to be aggressive and somewhat controversial. Not to the degree that GC tends to be, but you want around 1/3 of town to SR you - enough to not be NK'ed, but not enough to be hanged. I usually do this (intentionally or not) and the result is I often go deep into games, and that can be critical at LyLo (you don't want all the experienced town players dead then, ideally)

Counterpoint to Ben because why not:

You can also go deep in semiopen and closed setups by being likely to have a doctor on you. To do that you should try to make the maximum number of players townread you the maximum amount. (Within reason of course.) I have found this to be a much more comfortable way to live several days then almost being hung.
